titleStmt.title: Letter from Sir Thomas Little Heath to the Under Secretary of Ireland, 29 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Thomas Little Heath

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from Sir Thomas Little Heath (1861-1940) to the under secretary for Ireland concerning the case of Joshua Allen, a clerk in the employment of the solicitor to the Irish Land Commissioners. Heath requests that Chalmers inform the Lords Justices that since Allen appears to have been in the personal employment of the solicitor and paid out of the allowance to that solicitor for clerical assistance, Allen is ineligible for the award of a gratuity under Section 4 of the Superannuation Act, 1887.Sir Thomas Little Heath was a British civil servant. From 1913 to 1919 he occupied the position of permanent secretary to the Treasury.At the time this letter was written Sir Robert Chalmers (1858-1938) held the position of under secretary for Ireland. Chalmers was sent to Ireland as replacement under secretary for Sir Matthew Nathan, who had resigned along with Augustine Birrell, his chief secretary, following the outbreak of the Easter Rising in 1916.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papaers, CSO RP/1916-5620-13171

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
TREASURY CHAMBERS. 29 July 1916. Sir, In reply to Sir. E. O' Farrell's letter of the
29th ultimo (10994), relative to the case of Mr. Joshua
Allen, a Clerk in the employment of the Solicitor to
the Irish Land Commissioners, I am directed by the
Lords Commissioners of His Majesty's Treasury to request
you to inform the Lords Justices that, as this man
appears to have been in the personal employment of the
Solicitor and to have been paid out of the Allowance to
the Solicitor for clerical assistance, My Lords are
unable to regard him as eligible for the award of a
gratuity under Section 4 of the Superannuation Act, 1887. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, T. L. Heath The Under Secretary, Dublin Castle. I Send copy of this letter
to Land Commission
II F.D.
I.J.T.
1â8â16
